perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information shall not apply with respect to any Confidential Information to the extent the Receiving Party can demonstrate that such Confidential Information: (a) is generally known to the public at the time of disclosure or becomes generally known through no breach of this Agreement; (b) is in the Receiving Party’s rightful possession at the time of disclosure without an obligation to hold it in Page 2 of 2 confidence; (c) becomes known to the Receiving Party without obligation of confidence through disclosure by a third party h aving the legal right to disclose such information; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without access or reference to, or reliance upon, the Confidential Information ; or (e) is or becomes disclosed through the ordinary course of the NDA Purpose or as an element of any marketing plan intended to be released in the public domain or to targeted geographic public domain .
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information shall not apply with respect to any Confidential Information to the extent the Receiving Party can demonstrate that such Confidential Information: (a) is generally known to a select group of individuals at the time of disclosure or becomes generally known through no breach of this Agreement; (b) is in the Receiving Party’s rightful possession at the time of disclosure without an obligation to hold it in Page 2 of 2 confidence; (c) becomes known to the Receiving Party without obligation of confidence through disclosure by a third party h aving the legal right to disclose such information; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without access or reference to, or reliance upon, the Confidential Information ; or (e) is or becomes disclosed through the ordinary course of the NDA Purpose or as an element of any marketing plan intended to be released in the public domain or to targeted geographic public domain .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'generally known to the public' to 'generally known to a select group of individuals' weakens the exception for confidential information. This means information known to a small circle is still considered unprotected under this exception. This weakens the Receiving Party's obligation. However, earlier Section 2 stated confidential information is what is 'not generally known', creating a contradiction on what is protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information” shall mean all information that is not generally known or readily ascertainable by proper means by other persons and is either (i) des ignated as “confidential” or “proprietary” by the Disclosing Party prior to or at the time of disclosure or (ii) information that a reasonable person should recognize as being confidential or proprietary, based solely on the explicit written designation by the disclosing party. By way of example, Confidential Information includes, without limitation, information regarding a Party’s products, product development, services, agreements and contracts, business operations, business plans and financial affairs; employees and other personnel; vendors and customers; marketing plans and strategies; processes, compensation formulas, methods and specifications; and information technology network. Additionally, t he fact of the Parties discussions regarding a p otential business relationship shall be deemed Confidential Information subject to the protections of this Agreement. Confidential Information may be either the property of the Disclosing Party or information provided to the Disclosing Party by a third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the exception for confidential information from 'generally known to the public' to 'generally known to a select group of individuals'. This weakens the protection of confidential information. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' as information 'that is not generally known'. Therefore, the change creates a contradiction because the changed text allows information known to a select group to fall outside the definition of confidential information, directly conflicting with the contradicted text's definition.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date set f orth below and shall continue for a period of twelve (12) months thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date set f orth below and shall continue for a period of twelve (12) months, unless otherwise terminated with 30-day notice by the Candidate, thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: The original states the agreement is for 12 months. The changed text says 12 months unless terminated by the candidate. Section 5 sets the term. The 12 month term is now questionable, contradicting the certainty initially set.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Scope and Term of Agreement. This Agreement pertains to all Confidential Information that is disclosed o r otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates to the Receiving Party in connection with the NDA Purpose, whether prior to or during the term of this Agreement. The term of this Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date set f orth below and shall continue for a period of twelve (12) months, unless otherwise terminated with 30-day notice by the Candidate, thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a fixed term of 12 months. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing the Candidate to terminate the agreement with 30-day notice, thus creating ambiguity and contradicting the initial fixed term. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text in this case, repeats this contradiction, stating the agreement is for 12 months unless terminated. The 'unless' clause introduces an ambiguity making the term inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, return to the Disclos ing Party or, at the Disclosing Party’s direction, destroy all documents and any and all other tangible manifestations of Confidential Information received or created by the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement (and all copies and reproductions there of).
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, make reasonable efforts to return to the Disclos ing Party or, at the Disclosing Party’s direction, destroy all documents and any and all other tangible manifestations of Confidential Information received or created by the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement (and all copies and reproductions there of).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall return' to 'make reasonable efforts to return' weakens the obligation of the Receiving Party. The original text mandates return/destruction. The modified text makes this less definite, by using the expression 'make reasonable efforts'. Contradicts any later enforcement of the terms of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure and Protection of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall (i) hold all Confidential Information in confidence, and shall not disclose Confidential Information except as expressly permitted under the terms of this Agreement and (ii) use Confidential Information only for the NDA Purpose and for no other purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Receiving Party 'shall return' confidential information. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'make reasonable efforts to return'. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states 'The Receiving Party shall (i) hold all Confidential Information in confidence...' The modified text creates a loophole where the Receiving Party might not fully comply with their obligation to protect Confidential Information, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: AGX and CANDIDATE desire to exchange information and to engage in discussions concerning AGX’s agent program and Candidate’s qualifications to establish a freight Agency Relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: AGX and CANDIDATE may consider the possibility of exchanging information and might engage in tentative discussions concerning AGX’s agent program and Candidate’s qualifications to perhaps establish a freight Agency Relationship.
perturbation.explanation: The original text implies a clear intention to exchange information and discuss a relationship. The modified text weakens this intention with 'may consider', 'might engage', and 'perhaps', making the purpose of the NDA uncertain. The weakening here of the term, make the previous strong terms such as 'ensure' a contradiction to the changes
perturbation.contradicted_text: Background. AGX and CANDIDATE may consider the possibility of exchanging information and might engage in tentative discussions concerning AGX’s agent program and Candidate’s qualifications to perhaps establish a freight Agency Relationship. In the course of such discussions and negotiations, it is anticipated that a Party (“Disclosing Party”) may disclose or otherwise make available to the other Party (“Receiving Party”) certain confidential and proprietary inform ation for the purpose of enabling the Receiving Party to evaluate and the prospective business relationship (the “NDA Purpose”). The Parties have entered into this Agreement in order to ensure the confidentiality and proper protection of such confidential and proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The original text indicates a firm intent to exchange information and discuss a potential agency relationship. However, the changed text weakens this intent using terms like 'may consider,' 'might engage,' and 'perhaps,' thereby introducing ambiguity about the purpose of the NDA. The contradicted text states that the parties entered into the agreement 'in order to ensure the confidentiality and proper protection of such confidential and proprietary information.' The weakened language in the changed text directly contradicts the 'ensure' statement, as the purpose is no longer definitively to ensure confidentiality but merely to 'perhaps' consider it, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined under Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement pertains to all Confidential Information that is disclosed o r otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates to the Receiving Party in connection with the NDA Purpose, whether prior to or during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement pertains to Confidential Information that is explicitly identified in writing as such and disclosed o r otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates to the Receiving Party in connection with the NDA Purpose, specifically during the first six months of the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Original text states agreement pertains to ALL confidential information whether prior or during. The modified text limits the scope by stating 'explicitly identified in writing' and 'specifically during the first six months'. The amendment creates an ambiguity when considering the original text that states any confidential information being confidential and protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Scope and Term of Agreement. This Agreement pertains to Confidential Information that is explicitly identified in writing as such and disclosed o r otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates to the Receiving Party in connection with the NDA Purpose, specifically during the first six months of the term of this Agreement. The term of this Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date set f orth below and shall continue for a period of twelve (12) months, unless otherwise terminated with 30-day notice by the Candidate, thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement pertains to ALL confidential information, whether prior to or during the term of the agreement. The changed text limits this to confidential information 'explicitly identified in writing' and 'specifically during the first six months'. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but by limiting the scope of what is considered confidential, the changed text directly contradicts the original text's broader definition, creating a clear inconsistency and thus, a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.
